name: Misdirection
summary: Visual or attentional design that draws the user toward one option while another, more important option is downplayed or obscured.

evidence.claim: Misdirection appears in Brignull's classic dark-patterns taxonomy and the FTC 2022 'Bringing Dark Patterns to Light' report under deceptive design.

body.description: A high-contrast, brightly colored CTA pulls the eye toward 'Subscribe' or 'Buy Now', while the equivalent decline is rendered in low-contrast gray text styled as a link rather than a button. The user's gaze and click follow the design hierarchy, not the legal-equivalent choice.

detectionHeuristics.description: Compare visual weight (color saturation, size, font weight) of the primary CTA vs the equivalent decline. Flag when ratio exceeds a threshold.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Given a modal or choice surface, identify the primary action and the equivalent decline. Are they given equal visual weight, or does design hierarchy steer toward one?
body.remediation: Surface the secondary option with equal prominence in a Lens overlay. Recommend the user read the choice text rather than follow the visual hierarchy.
